impact: The login landing page uses `eve-border eve-glow rounded`, `bg-eve-surface`, `text-eve-muted`, `text-eve-accent`, and crucially `bg-blue-400` on the primary CTA (index.html:39). The in-file `tailwind.config` (base.html:11-32) declares `eve.bg/surface/border/accent/gold/danger/success/muted` — so `eve-border` (no hyphen prefix in config) and `eve-glow` (no such color at all) resolve to nothing. The page only looks correct because the CDN's JIT silently emits empty rules and the underlying b-* system provides the dark surface. The `bg-blue-400` is plain Tailwind blue — inconsistent with the gold (`--accent: #c8a951`) used everywhere else in the app, breaking brand on the FIRST page logged-out visitors see. Recommended: rewrite index.html using the b-* class system (matches the rest of the app), or correctly use `bg-eve-accent`/`bg-eve-surface` Tailwind utilities, and definitely replace `bg-blue-400` with the gold accent on the CTA. Quick win, high visibility (it's the public login page).
resolution: Fixed in commit cd0676c. Replaced 4x `eve-border rounded p-3` with `border border-eve-border p-3` and the outer card's `eve-border eve-glow rounded` with `border border-eve-border` (Tailwind v4 generates border-eve-border from the --color-eve-border theme token). eve-glow had no theme token at all — dropped entirely. Primary CTA's `hover:bg-blue-400` (off-brand plain Tailwind blue on the login page) → `hover:opacity-90` with `transition-opacity`, keeping the gold accent through the hover state. Verified deploy: commit cd0676c, healthz 200.
title: index.html references undefined Tailwind classes and off-brand colors
